questions.question: A prime number is defined as a positive whole number greater than 1 that has:
options: Exactly two distinct factors: 1 and itself.
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself.

questions.question: What is the smallest prime number?
questions.explanation: 2 is the smallest prime number. 1 is not prime because it does not have two distinct factors.

questions.question: Why is the number 1 not prime?
options: Because it is an even number.
Because it only has one positive factor.
Because it can be divided by any number.
questions.explanation: A prime number must have exactly two distinct factors. Since 1 has only one positive factor (1), it is not a prime number.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers is a prime number?
questions.explanation: 15 is divisible by 3 and 5; 21 is divisible by 3 and 7; 27 is divisible by 3 and 9. 29 has no positive factors other than 1 and 29, so it is prime.

questions.question: How many even prime numbers are there in the entire set of real numbers?
questions.explanation: There is exactly one even prime number, which is 2. Any other even number is divisible by 2, meaning it has at least three factors and is therefore composite.
